subject: PanelDockingService.ShowDropPreview—IndexOutOfRangeException
body:
## Bug Report: Docking Preview Crash

**Operation:** `DockingTestPlayback.ShowDropPreview`
**Error:** `System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException` at `PanelDockingService.cs:2120`

### Stack Trace
```
at SquadDash.PanelDocking.PanelDockingService.GetTopInsertionRect(Int32 targetOrder, List`1 panelsInZone)
at SquadDash.PanelDocking.PanelDockingService.GetSlotScreenRect(SlotButtonViewModel slot) [line 1842]
at SquadDash.PanelDocking.PanelDockingService.GetDropPreviewRect(String sourcePanelId, DockZone targetZone, Int32 targetOrder) [line 1792]
at SquadDash.MainWindow.<>c__DisplayClass834_6.<ShowDockingTestPlaybackWindow>b__21() [line 12099]
```

### Issue
The `GetTopInsertionRect` method accesses `panelsInZone[targetOrder]` without validating that the list contains elements. When the top zone is empty, accessing by index fails.

### File Location
`SquadDash/PanelDocking/PanelDockingService.cs:2120`

### Related Task
This may relate to the mid-priority task: **[Docking] Refactor DockingMapBuilder to loop-based zone layout**

actions.label: Investigate and fix
actions.prompt: There's an IndexOutOfRangeException in PanelDockingService when showing drop preview during docking operations. The error occurs at line 2120 in GetTopInsertionRect() method when trying to access panelsInZone[targetOrder]. The method doesn't validate that panelsInZone is non-empty before accessing elements by index. Please investigate the issue, identify all similar unsafe indexing patterns in the docking code, fix them, and ensure the docking preview works correctly even with empty zones.
